proposals.rationale: Committee dispersion is moderate (0.67 agreement), with Bull, Bear, and Skeptic converging on 'high/crowded' pricing but diverging on whether the bottleneck duration justifies current multiples. Burden of proof is on change; the existing thesis — skilled electrical labor scarcity binding through 2027+ — is directionally intact but the near-term valuation extension is the dominant risk. EME/PWR/FIX/MYRG trading 16–79% above 200-DMA with YTD gains of 30–105% is a concrete, repeated signal across all three seats that the near-term scarcity is already discounted. The Bull's core non-consensus argu
proposals.variant_view: Consensus sees the shortage and has priced the near-term bind, but likely underestimates that the data-center capex cycle (hyperscaler build-outs running through 2027–2028) creates a *multi-year* backlog conversion runway — not a one-quarter pop. The real miss is margin duration: if electrician scarcity persists as a structural gating factor rather than a cyclical one, EME/PWR/FIX/MYRG can compound backlog-to-revenue conversion at elevated margins for longer than the current multiple implies. The catalyst is sequential quarters of backlog growth *and* margin expansion in 2H26 earnings, paired
proposals.bear_case: The steelmanned bear is that the entire trade is a crowded consensus that has already run. At 16–79% above 200-DMA, these names price in not just the shortage but an extended supercycle. Supply response is non-linear: apprenticeship completions, immigration policy shifts, or modular/prefab electrification methods could relieve bottlenecks faster than the bull assumes. More dangerously, demand destruction is real — hyperscaler capex is notoriously lumpy and subject to abrupt pause (see 2022 cloud CapEx cuts). kill_criterion.condition: Two consecutive quarters where EME, PWR, or FIX report backlog growth below 5% YoY AND gross margin compresses more than 150bps YoY, OR any major hyperscaler (MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL) cuts data-center CapEx guidance by more than 15% for the following fiscal year
